meta.title: wcoin.casino SEO/GEO content plan
meta.source: Whale Growth social-intel (real demand signals + clustered topics + competitor-gap insights)
meta.note: Snapshot; classifier backlog still re-judging + collection ongoing — high-intent pool will grow, re-export periodically.

positioning.what: On-chain solvency & safety intelligence platform for crypto casinos.
positioning.moat: Proactive, machine-verifiable on-chain data (proof of reserves, real-time solvency monitoring, predictive risk scoring) vs AskGamblers/Casino Guru reactive post-incident complaint boards.
positioning.content_thesis: Verify before you play — every page helps a player avoid risk BEFORE depositing, using verifiable on-chain data.
positioning.tone: Neutral, data-driven, no subjective accusations; label sources/confidence; trust-first.

entity_review_pages.rationale: Players search brand name + safety/payout; these pages are the most explicit-intent, easiest to rank, and most likely to be cited by AI answer engines as a factual source. Build FIRST. Keep neutral and data-backed.

page_types.h1: Does {entity} pay out? Withdrawal & payout reliability

page_template.answer_first: Open with a one-line verdict + as-of date + the on-chain data basis.

intent_clusters.name: Is this casino safe/legit (core safety check)
intent_clusters.search_intent: A player vetting a specific casino before depositing.
evidence: How can players identify crypto casinos that refuse to pay winners?
Best Crypto Casino Sites: What Players Should Look For Before Choosing a Platform
content_types: How to tell if a crypto casino is safe (guide)
intent_clusters.geo_note: Lead with a clear safe/at-risk verdict + data basis; answer-first wins AI citations.

intent_clusters.name: Proof of reserves / solvency (strongest differentiator — own it)
intent_clusters.search_intent: Players & informed users wanting to verify a casino actually holds funds.
evidence: The distinction between 'proof of reserves' and 'proof of custody' is critical
No competitor publishes machine-verifiable reserve proofs
content_types: Definitional/educational long-form (proof of reserves vs custody)
How to verify a crypto casino's reserves (tool page)
keywords: proof of reserves vs proof of custody
intent_clusters.geo_note: Clear definitions + comparison tables = high AI-citation frequency; this is an uncontested authority slot.

intent_clusters.name: Payout / withdrawal failures
intent_clusters.search_intent: Player hit (or fearing) a blocked/slow withdrawal.
evidence: a delayed withdrawal or failed deposit costs you trust
content_types: Signs a crypto casino will stall your withdrawal
intent_clusters.geo_note: Use real complaint phrasing as H2 questions; pair with on-chain reserve context.

intent_clusters.name: Scam detection / red flags
intent_clusters.search_intent: Player wary of a casino flagged as a scam.
evidence: How can players identify crypto casinos that refuse to pay
content_types: On-chain signals a crypto casino is about to exit-scam (checklist)
keywords: how to spot a casino exit scam
intent_clusters.geo_note: Checklist + verifiable signals; keep neutral/data-based, never bare accusation.

intent_clusters.name: Comparison / ranking by data (not votes)
intent_clusters.search_intent: Player choosing between casinos; wants an objective basis.
evidence: awards rely on votes and subjective evaluation, easily manipulated
compare the odds before you place a single wager
content_types: Data-driven comparison pages (by reserves / on-chain activity / complaint trend)
intent_clusters.geo_note: Structured comparison tables are the most AI-extractable format.

intent_clusters.name: Crypto / stablecoin payment safety
evidence: treat payment choice as a risk-control decision
users repeatedly lose deposits with no visibility
content_types: What to check before funding a crypto casino
intent_clusters.geo_note: Frame payment choice as risk-control; concrete checklist.

priority_topics.title: How to identify crypto casinos that refuse to pay winners
priority_topics.target_question: How can players identify crypto casinos that refuse to pay winners?

priority_topics.title: How fast & safe are stablecoin casino withdrawals
priority_topics.target_question: How fast and safe are stablecoin withdrawals from crypto casinos?

priority_topics.title: Payout speed vs support quality at crypto casinos
priority_topics.target_question: Is fast payout more important than responsive support at crypto casinos?

priority_topics.title: Choosing a multi-crypto / stablecoin casino safely
priority_topics.target_question: Which platforms accept the widest crypto range safely?

priority_topics.title: World Cup crypto betting platforms compared
priority_topics.target_question: What is the best crypto casino/sportsbook for World Cup bets?

competitor_gaps.theme: Reactive complaint board vs pre-deposit alert
competitor_gaps.gap: AskGamblers/Casino Guru only log incidents after funds are lost.
No competitor publishes auditable reserve proofs.

competitor_gaps.theme: Proof of reserves vs custody education gap
competitor_gaps.gap: Players conflate the terms; nobody explains clearly.

competitor_gaps.theme: Complaint rate not tied to live solvency
competitor_gaps.gap: Resolution % (e.g. BCGame 32%) not linked to reserves/activity.
competitor_gaps.our_angle: Complaint data x on-chain reserves -> predictive risk score.
Rankings driven by real on-chain transactions + reserves.

geo_guidelines: Answer-first: open each page with a direct verdict + as-of date + data basis.
FAQ / Q&A structure with FAQ schema; use real user question phrasings as H2s.
Definitional content for key terms (proof of reserves vs custody, solvency, RTP).
Cite verifiable data points (reserve figures, wallet addresses, snapshot timestamps) to raise factual-source weight.
One clear conclusion + supporting data per page; avoid vague hedging.
rollout_order: 1. Entity review pages (entity_review_pages) — highest explicit intent, easiest to rank + AI-cite.
